methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Zephyr Fusion is a pre-seed nuclear energy company founded by Galen Burke and Edward Hinson. The valuation universe consists of 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear energy companies, including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private comparables total, and filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yields fewer than the minimum three datapoints required for a reliable median calculation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise and mask the specific capital requirements and technology risk profiles of nuclear development-stage companies.

Without sufficient narrow peer data, a robust quantitative valuation range cannot be established from the available comparables. Further valuation work would require either: (1) expansion of the comparable set through deeper sourcing of pre-seed nuclear companies; (2) application of alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis if detailed unit economics and development timelines are available; or (3) benchmark analysis against early-stage capital deployment in adjacent deep-tech sectors with similar hardware and regulatory complexity. The key uncertainty remains the absence of pre-seed and seed-stage nuclear energy transactions with disclosed valuations.
